summary: Comprehensive testing of restructured ops management features completed. All 16 backend tests passed (100%). All UI flows verified for Quick Actions, Deployments with kit cards, and Inventory with role-based permissions.

issues: Dashboard nav card still shows 'Actions' label instead of 'Quick Actions' for managers - minor inconsistency with page title

action_items: Minor: Consider updating Dashboard.js line 28 to change 'Actions' label to 'Quick Actions' for consistency with page title
critical_code_review_comments: Quick Actions page correctly shows only Transfer/Damage/Request - NO shift controls (correct)
Helpful note on Quick Actions: 'To start a collection shift, go to Deployments and select your assigned BnB'
Deployments page correctly shows kit cards inside expanded deployment with Start/Pause/Resume/Stop buttons
Start Collection dialog correctly requires only SSD and Activity Type (deployment context auto-populated)
Inventory page correctly groups items by category (SSDs, General, etc.)
Manager role: sees Transfer + Damage buttons only (NO Edit/Delete) - correct
Admin role: sees Transfer + Damage + Edit + Delete + Add Item - correct
Transfer dialog supports kit→kit, kit→bnb, kit→station location types - correct
POST /api/shifts/start correctly requires deployment_id - context-aware shifts working
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - used existing data from previous iterations
context_for_next_testing_agent: Restructured features fully tested. Quick Actions has only Transfer/Damage/Request. Shift controls (Start/Pause/Resume/Stop) are now inside Deployments→Kit cards. Inventory grouped by category with role-based permissions (Manager: view+transfer+damage, Admin: full CRUD). Shifts are context-aware with deployment_id required.
passed_tests: Backend: Admin login with admin123
Backend: Manager login with test123
Backend: POST /api/shifts/start requires deployment_id (422 without it)
Backend: GET /api/shifts/by-deployment/{id} returns kit shifts dict
Backend: Items have category field
Backend: Create item with category
Backend: Manager cannot delete items (403)
Backend: Manager cannot edit items (403)
Backend: Manager can view items
Backend: Manager can create transfer events
Backend: Manager can report damage events
Backend: Transfer kit→kit works
Backend: Transfer kit→bnb works
Backend: Transfer kit→station works
Backend: Deployments have assigned_kits field
Backend: Create deployment with kits
Frontend: Quick Actions shows only Transfer/Damage/Request buttons (no shift controls)
Frontend: Quick Actions has note about going to Deployments for shifts
Frontend: Deployments calendar view with deployment dots
Frontend: Click deployment expands to show kit cards
Frontend: Kit card shows Start button (not started) or Pause/Stop (active)
Frontend: Start Collection dialog has SSD and Activity Type dropdowns
Frontend: Inventory grouped by category (SSDs, General)
Frontend: Manager sees Transfer + Damage buttons only
Frontend: Manager does NOT see Edit/Delete/Add buttons
Frontend: Admin sees all buttons (Transfer/Damage/Edit/Delete/Add)
Frontend: Add Item dialog has Category field
Frontend: Edit Item dialog has Category field
Frontend: Transfer dialog supports all location types (kit/bnb/station)
features_verified: Quick Actions: Transfer/Damage/Request only (no Start/End shift)
Deployments: Kit cards with Start/Pause/Resume/Stop inside deployment
Deployments: Start Collection dialog requires SSD + Activity Type only
Inventory: Items grouped by category (SSDs, Cameras, Gloves, Tools, General)
Inventory: Manager can view + transfer + report damage (no edit/delete)
Inventory: Admin has full CRUD access
Transfer: Supports kit→kit, kit→bnb, kit→station location types
Context-aware shifts: deployment_id required for shift start
